title: Sessions Joined as Crew
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS sessions FROM JoinACrew WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT Captain AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM JoinACrew WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY Captain ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {sessions} multicrew session(s) joined as crew member during the report period. Joining as crew is the opposite of captaining — you trusted someone else to lead while you operated their weapons systems or flew their ship-launched fighter. The captain breakdown shows whose ships you've spent the most time aboard.
details.help: JoinACrew Captain is the hosting commander's name. sessions drives the title as the total crewing sessions count.

tagLines: Crew membership session log compiled. Ships joined and captains flown with noted.
Someone else's ship, someone else's call. You flew gunner, fighter, or helm. All valid contributions.
The captains you flew with most are the ones who made it worth coming back. The log remembers.
